title: Traffic / Services page topology
description: JSON Schema for traffic.json — defines service flows, Docker containers, Traefik reverse proxy routes, and inter-service dependencies. This schema validates data used by the Traffic page view in the Lab Map application. Nodes represent services and infrastructure, edges represent data flows, and flows provide filterable categories for visualizing specific traffic paths.

flows.$comment: Array of traffic flow categories for filtering
flows.description: List of traffic flow definitions. Each flow represents a category of traffic (e.g., web traffic, DNS, monitoring) that can be filtered in the UI. Nodes and edges reference flow IDs to indicate which flows they participate in.

nodes.description: List of all nodes in the traffic page. Each node represents a service, container, or infrastructure component with its flow memberships and service configuration.

edges.$comment: Array of traffic flow connections between nodes
edges.description: List of all edges representing traffic flows between nodes. Each edge specifies flow memberships, protocols, ports, and TLS configuration.

flowMeta.$comment: Traffic flow category definition for UI filtering
flowMeta.description: A traffic flow category used to group related nodes and edges for filtering. The flow ID is referenced by nodes and edges to indicate participation.

id.description: Unique flow identifier. Must start with 'flow_' followed by lowercase alphanumeric and underscores (e.g., flow_web, flow_dns, flow_monitoring).

label.description: Human-readable flow label shown in the flow bar UI (e.g., Web Traffic, DNS, Monitoring)

route.description: A reverse proxy route mapping a domain to a backend service. Used for Traefik, nginx, or similar proxies.

domain.description: Domain or hostname for this route (e.g., grafana.example.com, *.local.example.com)

backend.description: Backend service URL or identifier (e.g., http://grafana:3000, nas-01:5000)

serviceEntry.$comment: Docker container/service definition with full details
serviceEntry.description: A Docker container or service configuration with image, port mappings, environment variables, and volume mounts.

vip.description: Virtual IP address if using macvlan or similar (can be null if not applicable)

serviceConfig.description: Service configuration for a node containing Traefik routes, Docker services, and inter-VLAN firewall rules.

routes.description: Array of reverse proxy routes (for VIP/Traefik nodes)

services.description: Array of services. Can be simple service names (strings) or full service entries with Docker details.

anyOf.description: Full service entry with Docker configuration

interVlanRules.description: List of inter-VLAN firewall rules (for router nodes)

nodeMeta.$comment: Node metadata - hostname, role, and service configuration
nodeMeta.description: Metadata for a traffic page node containing identification and service configuration.

hostname.description: Node hostname or identifier (e.g., traefik-vip, nas-01, k3s-master)

role.description: Brief role description (e.g., Reverse Proxy VIP, Primary NAS, K3s Control Plane)

hostedOn.description: Physical host if this is a VM or container (e.g., proxmox-01, k3s-worker-01)

serviceConfig.description: Service configuration including routes, Docker services, and firewall rules

node.$comment: Traffic page node definition - a service or infrastructure component
node.description: A traffic page node representing a service, container, or infrastructure component that participates in one or more traffic flows.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node (used in edge from/to references)

label.description: Display label shown on the graph

type.description: Node type determining visual style and icon. Must match a folder name in public/node-icons/.

flows.description: Array of flow IDs this node participates in. Node is visible when any of its flows are selected.

meta.description: Node metadata with hostname, role, and service configuration

edge.$comment: Traffic page edge definition - a data flow connection
edge.description: A traffic flow connection between two nodes, specifying protocol, ports, TLS, and flow memberships.

from.description: Source endpoint with required nodeId and optional port

to.description: Target endpoint with required nodeId and optional port

type.description: Edge type: wan (ISP), cdn (Cloudflare), proxy (reverse proxy), hosts (DNS), forward (port forward), dependency (service dep), internal (internal traffic)

flows.description: Array of flow IDs this edge participates in. Edge is visible when any of its flows are selected.

label.description: Display label for this edge (shown on hover)

arrows.description: Arrow direction for vis-network (e.g., to, from, to;from)

tls.description: Whether this connection uses TLS encryption

tlsTermination.description: Where TLS is terminated (e.g., cloudflare, traefik, backend)

port.description: Port number (can be string like '443' or integer like 443)

protocol.description: Protocol (e.g., HTTPS, HTTP, DNS, SSH, SMB)

domains.description: List of domains/hostnames carried by this connection

edgeEndpoint.description: Edge endpoint containing node reference and optional port identifier.
